implementation_summary.AC-UI-1: NpubQrModal.tsx gained a validityHint prop, rendered under the QR/value block behind the exported pure predicate shouldShowValidityHint(shareUrl, validityHint) — true only for an actual shareUrl-encoded pairing card, never a bare-npub or group-invite QR. profile.tsx passes copy.profile.shareCardValidityHint ('This code works for about 30 minutes.' / de equivalent). profile.shareCardDescription (the pre-existing text shown above the Share button, before the modal opens) was also retexted in both languages to mention the ~30-minute window, so the affordance is visible both before and during the share flow.
implementation_summary.AC-UI-2: MarmotContext.tsx's onPairingAckReceived callback (previously a console.info-only S3 stub) now calls resolveAndApplyPairingAdmissionDigest on every admission, which resolves the issuer's currently-active nonce via nonceStore.ts's new peek-only peekActiveNonce() (review-remediation — see below; NEVER mints/prunes, unlike getOrMintActiveNonce), counts distinct senders in pairingAck.ts's existing getPairingAckAdmissions() map for that nonce via countAdmissionsForActiveNonce, and — only once the count reaches 2 — shows a single Chakra toast (id 'pairing-admission-digest') via applyPairingAdmissionDigest, updating that SAME toast in place on every later admission for the same nonce rather than stacking a new one. A lone first admission, or an ack received when no code has been shared this session (peek returns null), produces no notification. Neither pairingAck.ts nor nonceStore.ts's pre-existing exports were modified — nonceStore.ts gained one new additive read-only accessor (see review_remediation).
implementation_summary.AC-UI-3: decodeCard (S1, AC-CODEC-4) rejects an unrecognized header version with the error string now exported as contactCard.ts's UNSUPPORTED_VERSION_ERROR constant (review-remediation — see below; was an inline literal at landing), which parseContactCard propagates unchanged. processContactInput.ts previously collapsed EVERY parse failure into error: 'invalid_npub'. It now special-cases that one constant into a new 'unsupported_version' error code (no other parse failure is affected — a tampered signature, truncated payload, or garbage input still maps to 'invalid_npub' exactly as before, verified by a dedicated regression test). add.tsx's getErrorMessage switch gained a case rendering the new friendly copy.contacts.addContactErrorUnsupportedVersion string instead of falling through to the generic invalid-npub message.
implementation_summary.AC-INTL-1: Added en+de Copy type/object entries for: contacts.addContactErrorUnsupportedVersion, contacts.pairingAdmissionDigest ((count) => string), profile.shareCardValidityHint; retexted profile.shareCardDescription (both languages) to mention the validity window. Reviewed S4's two placeholder-labeled keys (contacts.addContactPairingInFlight, profile.pairingNameSetupPrompt) per the ownership-ledger's finalization note — both already read naturally in the app's voice in both languages; left unchanged. app/tests/unit/cards/pairingUiCopy.i18n.test.ts asserts en/de presence, non-emptiness, non-copy-paste, and (for the AC-UI-1/2/3 keys specifically) the count/30-minute content requirements.

manual_validation.note: Computed real QR-version/module-count deltas via the app's own `qrcode` package rather than estimating: at ECC level 'L' (the current shareUrl branch, NpubQrModal.tsx line ~94), a max-length v1 signed card (134 raw bytes -> 202-char share URL) renders at QR version 7 (45x45 modules); the AC-CODEC-6-larger v2 card (+20 bytes -> 229-char share URL) renders at version 8 (49x49 modules) — one version step up, not a jump into a materially harder-to-scan range. Bumping to ECC 'M' for the v2 payload instead (the naive 'more redundancy = safer' instinct) would push it to version 9 (53x53 modules) — a DENSER code for no benefit, since higher ECC costs more modules for the same data. Conclusion: the existing shareUrl ? 'L' : 'M' choice at NpubQrModal.tsx line 94 is correct and was NOT changed; no ECC-level adjustment was needed for the larger v2 payload. What remains genuinely unautomatable in this repo's no-jsdom, non-visual unit-test setup is confirming a real phone camera reliably reads a 49x49-module code rendered at the modal's 280px display box from typical scanning distance — flagged for a manual check (or an S6 e2e visual/OCR-of-decoded-payload pass if one is added) before this epic ships.

verification.post_impl_note: No post-impl questions added — implementation surfaced no risk the pre-impl commitment set (VQ-S5-001..010) did not already cover; several pre-impl questions (VQ-S5-007/008/009) explicitly named the exact test evidence produced (real-decoder-driven unsupported_version fixture, 2-then-3-sender digest scenario, predicate-level render-gate test), so the audit floor is already met.
verification.lint: NO_LINT (no committed ESLint config; confirmed again this story)
verification.typecheck: clean (npx tsc --noEmit, zero diagnostics before and after this remediation round)

findings_addressed.summary: The already_exists -> pairingEcho sev-5 fix (S4) had no regression test in the new processContactInput.test.ts file this story introduced.
findings_addressed.fix: Added a new describe block ('already_exists pairingEcho regression (review-remediation)') to app/tests/unit/cards/processContactInput.test.ts with 6 real-codec-driven cases: (a) already_exists + a LIVE unexpired v2 pairing card -> pairingEcho present with the exact {issuerPubkeyHex, nonceHex, expiresAt}; (b) already_exists + an EXPIRED v2 card, a v1 card, and a bare npub -> pairingEcho undefined in all three; (c) a self-scan of a live v2 card (-> error 'self') and a garbage parse failure (-> error 'invalid_npub') both never carry pairingEcho. Built via the real encodeCardV2/addContactByNpub production seam (mirrors addContactCardWiring.test.ts's existing helper of the same shape), not a hand-rolled fixture. Complements, does not replace, the pre-existing equivalent coverage in addContactCardWiring.test.ts.

findings_addressed.summary: The admission-digest wiring resolved the issuer's active nonce via getOrMintActiveNonce(), which mints a fresh nonce (a store write) whenever the in-memory pointer is absent or expired. A grace-window ack (legitimately arriving 30-150 minutes after mint, since admissibility extends to expiresAt+2h) would therefore silently rotate the issuer's pairing nonce on ack receipt and the digest would then count against the wrong (freshly-minted, zero-match) nonce.
findings_addressed.fix: Added an additive, genuinely read-only accessor `peekActiveNonce(): StoredNonce | null` to app/src/lib/pairing/nonceStore.ts — returns the in-memory pointer as-is, never mints/persists/prunes; getOrMintActiveNonce's own behavior is completely unchanged. Extracted the digest wiring's 'resolve nonce then apply' step into a new exported function, resolveAndApplyPairingAdmissionDigest(admissions, peek, ...), whose `peek` parameter type (`() => {nonce: string} | null`) offers no mint capability at all — structurally, not just conventionally, preventing this bug class. MarmotContext.tsx's onPairingAckReceived callback now calls peekActiveNonce (via this wrapper) instead of getOrMintActiveNonce; if peek returns null (no code shared this session), the digest is a no-op. Added a 'does not mint' integration test suite to nonceStore.integration.test.ts (peek before any mint -> null; peek does not cause a subsequent getOrMintActiveNonce to observe a pre-existing nonce; peek past the active nonce's own expiry still returns the stale value rather than nothing/a fresh mint) plus a wiring-level test suite in admissionDigest.test.ts driving resolveAndApplyPairingAdmissionDigest directly with a null-returning and a stale-but-real fake peek.

findings_addressed.summary: processContactInput.ts hardcoded a local copy of the literal string 'contactCard: unsupported version' that contactCard.ts:529 also emits inline — a reworded S1 error message would silently desync the AC-UI-3 friendly-copy mapping from the codec that actually produces the error.
findings_addressed.fix: Exported the literal as `UNSUPPORTED_VERSION_ERROR` from app/src/lib/contactCard.ts (single source of truth) and used it at decodeCard's line-529 return site — the string VALUE is unchanged, this is purely a duplication fix. processContactInput.ts now imports and compares against that constant instead of its own local copy, which was deleted.

verification_after_remediation.full_suite: make test-unit — 2630 passed, 25 failed (the same pre-existing icon-set failures, unchanged), 2655 total

review_remediation.note: Two of the three fixes are additive-only edits to prior-story modules (contactCard.ts: S1, nonceStore.ts: S2), explicitly authorized by the remediation brief as review-remediation with no behavior change to either module's existing exports. modules_touched remains ["ui-i18n"] per this story's owning_module; the additive prior-module touches are recorded here rather than expanding modules_touched, since neither module's existing public contract, decision logic, or owned data changed — only a net-new read-only export was added to each.

retrospective.harder_than_needed: This session was handed 'implement Story S5' directly, with S1-S4 already sitting uncommitted in the working tree from prior sessions. Step 0's baseline capture is meant to run BEFORE any design/implementation, but by the time I ran `make test-unit` for the first time, I had already made this story's production-code edits (i18n.ts, NpubQrModal.tsx, MarmotContext.tsx, etc.) — there was no explicit checkpoint forcing the baseline run first, and reading the epic's dense cross-story context (architecture.md, exploration.json, ownership-ledger.json, S3/S4's own artifacts) before touching code felt like the natural first step but left no pristine pre-edit baseline to compare against. I captured the closest available approximation (post-production-edit, pre-new-test-files) and noted the gap in baseline.json rather than fabricating a pristine number.
retrospective.surprised_by: The admission-digest correctness bug (finding 2) came from reusing getOrMintActiveNonce purely for its read behavior without checking whether 'read while unexpired' was actually the ONLY path through that function — it mints on ANY absent/expired pointer, and the digest's own trigger (a received ack) is precisely the kind of event that tends to arrive well after a 30-minute active-nonce TTL has lapsed, given the 2h grace window. A function whose name says 'get or mint' should have been the giveaway; I read past it because the doc comment's AC-NONCE-1 framing ('reuses while unexpired') made the reuse case feel like the common one, when for THIS caller the expired-mint case was actually the likely one.

absorbed_from.note: Stage-1 Opus review surfaced 3 findings on the S5 diff: (sev4/testing) missing already_exists+pairingEcho regression coverage in the new processContactInput.test.ts; (sev3/correctness) the admission-digest wiring calling a minting primitive (getOrMintActiveNonce) instead of a read-only one, risking a nonce-rotation-on-ack-receipt bug; (sev3/best_practices) processContactInput.ts duplicating contactCard.ts's error-string literal by value instead of importing a shared constant. All three applied as additive-only fixes (finding 2 and 3 touched prior-story modules S1/S2 — export-only, no behavior change to existing exports).
